records.ety: [OE. waiten, OF. waitier, gaitier, to watch, attend, F. guetter to watch, to wait for, fr. OHG. wahta a guard, watch, G. wacht, from OHG. wahhēn to watch, be awake. √134. See Wake, v. i.]
records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Waited; p. pr. & vb. n. Waiting.]

defs: To watch; to observe; to take notice.
quotes: “But [unless] ye wait well and be privy, I wot right well, I am but dead,” quoth she.

defs: To stay or rest in expectation; to stop or remain stationary till the arrival of some person or event; to rest in patience; to stay; not to depart.
quotes: All the days of my appointed time will I wait, till my change come.
They also serve who only stand and wait.
Haste, my dear father; 't is no time to wait.

defs: To stay for; to rest or remain stationary in expectation of; to await; as, to wait orders.
quotes: Awed with these words, in camps they still abide, And wait with longing looks their promised guide.

defs: To attend as a consequence; to follow upon; to accompany; to await.
To attend on; to accompany; especially, to attend with ceremony or respect.
quotes: He chose a thousand horse, the flower of all His warlike troops, to wait the funeral.
Remorse and heaviness of heart shall wait thee, And everlasting anguish be thy portion.

defs: To cause to wait; to defer; to postpone; -- said of a meal; as, to wait dinner.

records.ety: [OF. waite, guaite, gaite, F. guet watch, watching, guard, from OHG. wahta. See Wait, v. i.]

defs: The act of waiting; a delay; a halt.
quotes: There is a wait of three hours at the border Mexican town of El Paso.

defs: Hautboys, or oboes, played by town musicians; not used in the singular.
Musicians who sing or play at night or in the early morning, especially at Christmas time; serenaders; musical watchmen.
quotes: The sound of the waits, rude as may be their minstrelsy, breaks upon the mild watches of a winter night with the effect of perfect harmony.
